# Stack Review Checklist

## Python Conventions
- [ ] Type hints on all public functions/methods
- [ ] `from __future__ import annotations` at top of each file
- [ ] No `async`/`await` — use `threading.Event` for concurrency

## CLI Conventions (click)
- [ ] Entry point: `spectral = "spectral.cli:main"` in pyproject.toml
- [ ] Options use `--duration`/`-d` for seconds
- [ ] Mood argument uses `click.Choice(list(MOODS.keys()))`

## Audio Conventions
- [ ] `SAMPLE_RATE = 44100` used from `engine.py` constant — no hardcoding
- [ ] Audio callbacks are non-blocking and allocation-free
- [ ] Buffer pre-allocation implemented for audio callbacks

## Package Layout
- [ ] Source layout: `src/spectral/` package
- [ ] `__init__.py` contains version only
- [ ] `moods.py` is pure data — no I/O, no imports from engine
- [ ] `engine.py` handles audio threading
- [ ] `cli.py` handles all user-facing I/O via `rich`

## Testing
- [ ] Tests live in `tests/` directory
- [ ] Run with `python3 -m pytest`
- [ ] Audio output tests use mocking or fallback mode

## Build & Distribution
- [ ] `hatchling` as build backend in pyproject.toml
- [ ] Dependencies declared: click>=8.1, rich>=13.0, sounddevice>=0.4, numpy>=1.26, scipy>=1.11
- [ ] `requires-python = ">=3.11"`

## Dependencies Check
- [ ] `sounddevice` import guarded (ImportError, OSError)
- [ ] Fallback prints warning and sleeps when audio unavailable
- [ ] `numpy` used for all audio buffer operations
- [ ] `scipy` declared but not yet used [?]
